title: mentha arvensis subsp. piperascens
intro: Mentha arvensis subsp. piperascens, also known as Japanese mint, is a subspecies of the wild mint plant native to Japan and parts of Asia. It is a perennial herb with fragrant leaves and stems that have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The health benefits of Japanese mint include relief from nausea, headaches, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cognitive function. Japanese mint can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate a range of health complaints.
